subject: [PATCH v5 06/36] cpuset: exclude private nodes from cpuset.mems (default-open)

2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Linux kernel's cpuset functionality to exclude private nodes from being managed by cpusets. Private nodes are treated as globally accessible resources, rather than being partitioned among cpusets. The patch also ensures that memory policies are re-bound when a private node leaves N_MEMORY_PRIVATE state on hot-unplug. This change aims to prevent issues with silent background operations and migrations due to cpuset membership changes.
